description: Content that incorporates Islamic greetings, blessings, and religious expressions to convey goodwill and cultural alignment during religious occasions and celebrations.
structural_recipe: Open with Islamic greeting or blessing phrase (e.g., 'مع أطيب التهاني' - with best wishes). Include dua/supplication language requesting Allah's acceptance of deeds. Reference specific Islamic occasion (Eid, Ramadan). Add relevant emoji (✨). Include branded hashtags alongside religious occasion hashtags (#عيدـالفطر). Maintain formal yet warm tone.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences have deep religious identity and values. Religious sentiment in messaging demonstrates brand respect for Islamic principles and cultural identity. During Islamic occasions, audiences expect and emotionally respond to genuine religious acknowledgment. This pattern builds trust and perceived cultural authenticity with predominantly Muslim consumer base.
transplantation_caveats: Religious expressions must be grammatically correct and authentic Arabic Islamic phrasing—errors damage credibility
Only deploy during appropriate Islamic occasions; off-season usage appears inauthentic
Requires genuine brand alignment with religious values; cynical or commercial-only usage is detected negatively
Non-Muslim brands must exercise caution to avoid perceived appropriation or disrespect
Avoid mixing religious sentiment with unrelated promotional content in same post
cultural_constraints: Islamic authenticity is non-negotiable; shallow religiosity alienates audience
Timing to religious calendar (Hijri) is critical for relevance
Gender considerations in imagery/messaging when religious references present
Avoid depicting religious practices incorrectly or irreverently
